notes: Public-source v0.1 database for the global aerospace stack.
This is a strategic dependency graph, not a supplier-contract database.
Edges marked fact_status=inferred are system-level dependency hypotheses.
Focus areas: OEMs, engines, tier-1 suppliers, MRO, certification, space, air traffic operations, sustainability and software/data opportunities.
Designed for Codex ingestion and website graph generation.

airbus_commercial.title: Airbus — Commercial Aircraft
airbus_commercial.notes: Airbus designs, builds and supports commercial aircraft; portfolio includes passenger aircraft, freighters and services.

airbus_space.title: Airbus — Space
airbus_space.notes: Airbus space portfolio: Earth observation, telecom/navigation satellites, exploration, ground systems, space equipment and support.

airbus_defence.title: Airbus — Defence
airbus_defence.notes: Airbus defence portfolio: military aircraft, UAS, FCAS, military space, connectivity, intelligence/data.

boeing_commercial.title: Boeing — Commercial
boeing_commercial.notes: Boeing commercial aircraft portfolio includes 737 MAX, 777, 777X, 787 and freighters.

boeing_space.title: Boeing — Space
boeing_space.notes: Boeing space systems: satellites, launch/space systems and government space products.

boeing_services.title: Boeing — Services
boeing_services.notes: Boeing Global Services supports commercial and government aircraft, sustainment and training.

safran_aerospace.title: Safran — Aerospace
safran_aerospace.notes: Safran aerospace portfolio: aircraft engines, equipment, interiors, landing systems, electrical and avionics.

safran_engines.title: Safran Aircraft Engines
safran_engines.notes: Safran Aircraft Engines develops, produces and supports aircraft engines.

cfm.title: CFM International
cfm.notes: CFM International is the 50/50 joint company of GE Aerospace and Safran Aircraft Engines.

ge_aerospace_commercial.title: GE Aerospace — Commercial Aircraft Engines & Services
ge_aerospace_commercial.notes: GE Aerospace commercial aircraft engines and services.

rolls_royce_civil.title: Rolls-Royce — Civil Aerospace
rolls_royce_civil.notes: Rolls-Royce civil aerospace products and services.

rtx_collins.title: Collins Aerospace
rtx_collins.notes: Avionics, aircraft systems, interiors, mission systems and aerospace technology.

rtx_raytheon.title: Raytheon
rtx_raytheon.notes: RTX business with air, land, sea, space, integrated air defense and advanced technology capabilities.

honeywell_aerospace.title: Honeywell Aerospace Technologies
honeywell_aerospace.notes: Aerospace products, services, software, avionics, navigation, connectivity, engines/APU support and customer portal.

thales_avionics.title: Thales — Aerospace
thales_avionics.notes: Aerospace avionics, connectivity, air traffic management, flight deck and systems.

leonardo_aeronautics.title: Leonardo — Aeronautics
leonardo_aeronautics.notes: Leonardo aeronautics portfolio: aircraft, UAVs, aerostructures and support.

dassault_group.title: Dassault Aviation — Group
dassault_group.notes: Dassault Aviation corporate and aircraft portfolio context.

embraer.title: Embraer
embraer.notes: Brazilian aerospace company producing commercial, executive and defense aircraft.

bombardier.title: Bombardier
bombardier.notes: Business jet manufacturer and services provider.

textron_aviation.title: Textron Aviation
textron_aviation.notes: Cessna, Beechcraft and Hawker aircraft ecosystem.

spacex.title: SpaceX
spacex.notes: Launch, spacecraft and Starlink satellite constellation company.

blue_origin.title: Blue Origin
blue_origin.notes: Launch vehicles, engines and space systems.

arianegroup.title: ArianeGroup
arianegroup.notes: European space launch and propulsion company.

easa.title: EASA — European Union Aviation Safety Agency

eutelsat.title: Eutelsat Group
eutelsat.notes: European satellite operator, including GEO/LEO connectivity after OneWeb combination.

thales_alenia_space.title: Thales Alenia Space
thales_alenia_space.notes: Satellite systems and space infrastructure joint venture.

mtu.title: MTU Aero Engines
mtu.notes: German engine manufacturer and MRO provider.

liebherr_aerospace.title: Liebherr Aerospace
liebherr_aerospace.notes: Aircraft systems supplier including landing gear, flight control, air management and actuation systems.

parker_aerospace.title: Parker Aerospace
parker_aerospace.notes: Motion and control systems for aerospace.

sita.title: SITA
sita.notes: Air transport IT and communications provider.

aerospike_placeholder.title: Placeholder — Aero supply chain sources to enrich
aerospike_placeholder.notes: Several supplier relations should be enriched with annual reports and official partner pages in v0.2.

nodes.name: Global Aerospace Stack
nodes.description: Global aerospace strategic dependency graph across aircraft, engines, space, regulation, suppliers, MRO and opportunity zones.

nodes.name: Airframer / OEM Layer
nodes.description: Commercial, business, defence and regional aircraft manufacturers.

nodes.name: Engine / Propulsion Layer
nodes.description: Turbofan, turboprop, rotorcraft, launch and future propulsion systems.

nodes.name: Aerostructures / Materials Layer
nodes.description: Fuselage, wings, composite structures, forgings, castings, titanium, aluminum and advanced materials.

nodes.name: Avionics / Flight Systems Layer
nodes.description: Avionics, cockpit systems, flight controls, navigation, communication, sensors and software assurance.

nodes.name: Landing Gear / Actuation Layer
nodes.description: Landing gear, braking, hydraulic, electrical, actuation and motion-control systems.

nodes.name: Cabin / Interiors Layer
nodes.strategic_role: Cabin systems and passenger experience layer
nodes.description: Seats, cabin systems, galleys, lavatories, connectivity and passenger experience equipment.

nodes.name: MRO / Aftermarket Layer
nodes.description: Engine shop visits, spares, repairs, component maintenance, line maintenance, predictive maintenance and lifecycle services.

nodes.name: Certification / Regulation Layer
nodes.description: Type certification, continued airworthiness, safety rules, software certification and regulator approval.

nodes.name: Air Traffic / Airline Operations Layer
nodes.description: Air traffic management, airline operations, dispatch, flight planning, airport constraints, slots and connectivity.

nodes.name: Space Launch Layer
nodes.description: Launch vehicles, launch services, propulsion, pads, range access and mission integration.

nodes.name: Satellite / Space Systems Layer
nodes.description: Satellites, payloads, satellite buses, space systems, constellations and ground segments.

nodes.name: Sustainable Aviation Layer
nodes.description: SAF, hydrogen, hybrid-electric, electric aircraft, contrails, lightweighting and operational efficiency.

nodes.name: Aerospace Software / Data Layer
nodes.description: Digital thread, PLM, simulation, certification evidence, health monitoring, flight data, cybersecurity and operational analytics.

nodes.name: Aerospace Supply Chain Visibility Layer
nodes.description: Multi-tier supplier visibility, lead times, quality escapes, capacity, certification status and geopolitical exposure.

nodes.name: EASA
nodes.description: EU aviation safety agency responsible for safety regulation/certification in Europe.

nodes.name: FAA
nodes.description: US aviation regulator responsible for civil aviation safety and certification.

nodes.name: ICAO
nodes.description: International Civil Aviation Organization creates international civil aviation standards and recommended practices.

nodes.name: IATA
nodes.description: Global airline trade association relevant to airline operations, safety, sustainability and commercial aviation demand.

nodes.name: EUROCONTROL
nodes.description: European air traffic management organization.

nodes.name: ESA
nodes.description: European space agency and major programme/customer node for space systems.

nodes.name: NASA
nodes.description: US space agency and major space systems/customer node.

nodes.name: Airbus
nodes.description: Commercial aircraft, helicopters, defence and space; designs, builds and supports aircraft.

nodes.name: Boeing
nodes.description: Commercial aircraft, defence, space and services.

nodes.name: Embraer
nodes.description: Brazilian aerospace OEM in commercial, executive and defence aircraft.

nodes.name: COMAC
nodes.description: Chinese commercial aircraft manufacturer and strategic challenger to Airbus/Boeing.

nodes.name: Dassault Aviation
nodes.description: Business jets and defence aircraft; strong French strategic aerospace node.

nodes.name: Bombardier
nodes.description: Business jet manufacturer and services provider.

nodes.name: Gulfstream Aerospace
nodes.description: Business aviation aircraft manufacturer.

nodes.name: Textron Aviation
nodes.description: Cessna, Beechcraft and Hawker aircraft ecosystem.

nodes.name: Leonardo Aeronautics
nodes.description: Aircraft, UAVs, aerostructures and support.

nodes.name: GE Aerospace
nodes.description: Commercial aircraft engines and services; major global propulsion node.

nodes.name: Safran Aircraft Engines
nodes.description: Aircraft engines and support; Safran group engine node.

nodes.name: CFM International
nodes.description: 50/50 joint company of GE Aerospace and Safran Aircraft Engines; major narrowbody engine ecosystem.

nodes.name: Rolls-Royce Civil Aerospace
nodes.description: Civil aerospace engines and services.

nodes.name: Pratt & Whitney
nodes.description: Aircraft engines and propulsion systems.

nodes.name: MTU Aero Engines
nodes.description: Engine modules, components and maintenance/MRO services.

nodes.name: ArianeGroup
nodes.description: Ariane launch vehicle and space propulsion ecosystem.

nodes.name: Blue Origin
nodes.description: Launch vehicles, engines and space systems.

nodes.name: Collins Aerospace
nodes.description: Aerospace systems supplier across avionics, interiors, mission systems and aircraft systems.

nodes.name: Honeywell Aerospace
nodes.description: Aerospace products, services, software, navigation, support and customer portal.

nodes.name: Thales Aerospace
nodes.description: Aerospace avionics, connectivity, air traffic management and flight systems.

nodes.name: Safran
nodes.description: Engines, equipment, landing systems, interiors and avionics/electrical systems.

nodes.name: Liebherr Aerospace
nodes.description: Landing gear, flight control, air management and actuation systems.

nodes.name: Parker Aerospace
nodes.description: Aerospace motion and control systems.

nodes.name: Moog Aircraft
nodes.description: Aircraft flight control and actuation systems.

nodes.name: Spirit AeroSystems
nodes.description: Aerostructures supplier for major aircraft programmes.

nodes.name: GKN Aerospace
nodes.description: Aerospace systems and structures supplier.

nodes.name: Leonardo Aerostructures
nodes.description: Aerostructures and aircraft manufacturing support.

nodes.name: SITA
nodes.description: Airline/airport communications and IT infrastructure provider.

nodes.name: Satcom Direct
nodes.description: Business aviation connectivity and data services.

nodes.name: Flightradar24
nodes.description: Flight tracking data and operational visibility platform.

nodes.name: SpaceX
nodes.description: Launch services, spacecraft and Starlink constellation.

nodes.name: Rocket Lab
nodes.description: Small launch and space systems provider.

nodes.name: Arianespace
nodes.description: European commercial launch services provider.

nodes.name: Eutelsat Group
nodes.description: European satellite connectivity operator including GEO/LEO assets.

nodes.name: SES
nodes.description: Satellite communications provider.

nodes.name: Intelsat
nodes.description: Satellite communications provider.

nodes.name: Thales Alenia Space
nodes.description: Satellite and space infrastructure systems company.

nodes.name: OHB
nodes.description: Space systems and technology group.

nodes.name: Airbus Space Systems
nodes.description: Airbus space products and services including satellites, exploration, ground systems and equipment.

nodes.name: Boeing Space Systems
nodes.description: Boeing space systems and satellites.

nodes.name: Engine MRO Capacity
nodes.description: Engine maintenance, spare parts, shop slots and lifecycle support capacity.

nodes.name: Castings / Forgings Capacity
nodes.description: Large castings, forgings, discs, blades, shafts, structural parts and qualified supplier capacity.

nodes.name: Titanium / Specialty Metals Dependency
nodes.description: Titanium, nickel superalloys, aluminum, composites, resins and specialty metals supply exposure.

nodes.name: Semiconductor / Avionics Electronics Dependency
nodes.description: Flight computers, avionics, sensors, navigation, comms and supply of certified electronics.

nodes.name: Certification Bottleneck
nodes.description: Type certification, software certification, continued airworthiness, production approval and regulatory capacity.

nodes.name: Skilled Aerospace Workforce
nodes.description: Engineers, technicians, machinists, maintenance staff, certification specialists and software assurance talent.

nodes.name: Quality Escape / Traceability Risk
nodes.description: Non-conformance, counterfeit parts, paperwork gaps, supplier quality escapes and traceability failures.

nodes.name: SAF Supply Constraint
nodes.description: SAF availability, feedstock, certification, price and logistics constraints.

nodes.name: Hydrogen Infrastructure Gap
nodes.description: Hydrogen production, storage, airport infrastructure, safety, certification and aircraft integration.

nodes.name: Aviation Cybersecurity Risk
nodes.strategic_role: Connected aircraft and operations cyber risk
nodes.description: Aircraft software, airline operations, connectivity, satcom, ground systems and maintenance data cyber exposure.

nodes.name: Airspace / Airport Capacity Constraint
nodes.description: Airspace congestion, airport slots, ATM modernization and operational disruption exposure.

nodes.name: Launch Cadence / Range Constraint
nodes.description: Launch cadence, range availability, propulsion, pads, payload integration and regulatory access.

opportunities.title: Aerospace supplier dependency and quality-risk graph
opportunities.reason: OEMs and Tier-1s are exposed to multi-tier supplier delays, quality escapes and traceability gaps. A graph-based supplier-risk system can map bottlenecks, certifications, lead times and capacity.

opportunities.title: Certification evidence OS for aerospace software and hardware
opportunities.reason: Certification is a structural bottleneck. A tool that manages evidence, requirements, traceability, testing artifacts and regulator-ready packages can serve OEMs, suppliers and startups.

opportunities.title: Engine MRO capacity and parts intelligence platform
opportunities.reason: Engine MRO capacity and spare parts are red bottlenecks for airline uptime. A platform can forecast shop visits, parts constraints, slot availability and fleet readiness.

opportunities.title: Aerospace spares and aftermarket FinOps
opportunities.reason: Aftermarket costs, parts scarcity and fleet availability drive airline economics. A spend/availability intelligence layer can optimize spares, repair decisions and contracts.

opportunities.title: Avionics and connected-aircraft cyber assurance layer
opportunities.reason: Aircraft, satcom, MRO and airline systems are increasingly connected. A cyber assurance layer for aviation software, connectivity, maintenance data and vendor risk has strong B2B value.

opportunities.title: SAF procurement and traceability platform
opportunities.reason: SAF adoption depends on supply availability, certification, carbon accounting and logistics. A platform can help airlines, airports and corporates manage SAF claims and procurement.

opportunities.title: Airspace disruption and airline operations intelligence
opportunities.reason: Congestion, weather, airspace constraints and airport capacity affect airline economics. A real-time operational intelligence layer can serve dispatch, network planning and risk teams.

opportunities.title: Launch access and satellite deployment intelligence
opportunities.reason: Satellite operators depend on launch cadence, pricing, range access and mission integration. A launch market intelligence tool can help satellite builders and operators plan deployment paths.

opportunities.title: Ground segment and satellite operations observability
opportunities.reason: Satellite constellations need ground segment, anomaly detection, network operations and data routing. A software layer can serve operators without building satellites.

opportunities.title: Digital thread for aerospace SMEs and suppliers
opportunities.reason: Aerospace suppliers struggle with requirements, changes, quality documents, configuration and traceability. A lightweight digital-thread tool can target SMEs below PLM giants.

opportunities.title: Hydrogen airport and aircraft-readiness assessment platform
opportunities.reason: Hydrogen aviation needs airports, storage, safety, certification and operations readiness. A planning platform can help airports, OEMs and public agencies prepare before aircraft arrive.

opportunities.title: Aerospace opportunity radar for founders and investors
opportunities.reason: The aerospace stack has hidden B2B opportunity zones across MRO, certification, supply chain, space, SAF, software and cyber. The graph itself can become a founder/VC opportunity engine.
